mission.title: hami: VRAM usage is shown differently on between node and pod
mission.description: VRAM usage is shown differently on between node and pod. Community-requested feature.

steps.title: Check current hami deployment
steps.description:
Verify your hami version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n hami -l app.kubernetes.io/name=hami
helm list -n hami 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working hami installation.

steps.title: Review hami configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant hami configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n hami -l app.kubernetes.io/name=hami
kubectl get configmap -n hami -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=hami
```
### 1. Issue or feature description
I used `nvidia-smi` to list the usage of gpu memory of pods and I encountered weird situation that the usage of process is shown differently on between node and pod.

steps.title: Apply the fix for VRAM usage is shown differently on between node and pod
Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n hami -l app.kubernetes.io/name=hami
kubectl get events -n hami --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "VRAM usage is shown differently on between node and pod" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary: This issue still exists, we will further debug and fix it
